title: Splash POC: File Watcher & Auto-Refresh
body:
## Problem Statement

When using Splash, developers often run harnesses (like shell commands, formatters, or agent scripts) that modify files in the Working Tree. Currently, if a modified file is open in a File Tab, Splash does not reflect those changes until the tab is manually closed and reopened, leading to stale reads and confusion.

## Solution

Splash will automatically watch the entire Working Tree for file modifications. When a file is modified externally, any open File Tab displaying that file will instantly and automatically reload its contents from disk, ensuring the TUI always displays the most up-to-date version of the file without manual intervention.

## User Stories

1. As a developer using Splash, I want a single recursive file watcher placed on the Working Tree at launch, so that Splash can detect modifications to any file in my project.
2. As a developer using Splash, I want rapid duplicate file modification events to be debounced, so that Splash doesn't aggressively reload a file multiple times for a single logical save or truncation.
3. As a developer using Splash, I want any open File Tab (whether active in the Main Pane or backgrounded in the Tab Bar) to reload its contents immediately when its underlying file is modified, so that my File Tabs are always fresh.
4. As a developer using Splash, I want my vertical scroll offset to be preserved when a File Tab auto-refreshes, so that my reading position isn't abruptly reset to the top of the file when a harness writes to it.
5. As a developer using Splash, I want the vertical scroll offset to be safely clamped to the new file length if the file gets shorter after an auto-refresh, so that I don't get stuck scrolling past the end of the file.

## Implementation Decisions

- Use the `notify-debouncer-mini` crate configured with a short timeout (e.g., 50ms to 100ms) to coalesce rapid filesystem events.
- Place a single recursive watch on the Working Tree (`std::env::current_dir()`) during application startup.
- Run the debouncer on a background thread that communicates with the main TUI event loop via a non-blocking channel (e.g., `std::sync::mpsc::channel`).
- In the main event loop (e.g., inside `SplashApp::tick()` or when `crossterm::event::poll` times out), check the channel for debounced events.
- For each modified file path in the events, search `SplashApp::tabs` for any `FileTab` instances matching that path.
- When reloading a `FileTabState`:
  - Reload the text lossily from disk (`String::from_utf8_lossy`).
  - Parse the new lines into the state.
  - Update `scroll_offset` to `scroll_offset.min(max_scroll)` based on the new line count.
- We do not manage adding/removing individual file watches when tabs open/close; we exclusively rely on the global Working Tree watcher and filter the incoming events.

## Testing Decisions

- **Test Philosophy**: Only test external behavior, avoiding tests that depend on internal private state.
- **Unit Seam (`src/app.rs`)**: 
  - Test `SplashApp::tick()` by injecting a mock channel receiver into `SplashApp`. 
  - Simulate a file modification event arriving on the channel.
  - Verify that `SplashApp` updates the `FileTabState` and properly clamps the `scroll_offset`.
- **Integration Seam (`tests/` using `TestHarness`)**: 
  - Add an offscreen headless snapshot test.
  - Launch a `TestHarness`, open a temporary file into a File Tab.
  - Write new data to the physical file using `std::fs` to trigger the physical file modification.
  - Wait for the debounced event to propagate.
  - Call `app.tick()` and use `assert_snapshot!` to verify the Main Pane displays the new contents without requiring keyboard input.

## Out of Scope

- File editing from within Splash (`Ctrl+S` is disabled as File Tabs are read-only). Hence, no "Disk Conflict" resolution mechanism is needed.
- Auto-refreshing the File Tree structure (this issue is purely about auto-refreshing open File Tabs).

## Further Notes

- `CONTEXT.md` terms adhered to: Working Tree, File Tab, Harness, Main Pane, Tab Bar.
